title: Add role-based portal login landing pages for each access category

requirements.text: Create a Portal Selection page at /portal (or as a prominent section on the homepage) that displays four distinct portal entry cards: Student Portal, Staff Portal, Admin Portal, and Parent Portal. Each card must have a clear label, a brief description of the portal, and a 'Login' or 'Access Portal' button. Clicking a card navigates the visitor to that role's dedicated login page.

quotes: once a visitor clicks on their category either as a staff student admin or alsoever if you take them to a page where they would login as that portal access category

acceptanceCriteria: A Portal Selection page exists with four cards: Student Portal, Staff Portal, Admin Portal, and Parent Portal.
Each card has a descriptive label and an action button.
Clicking a card navigates to that role's dedicated login page (e.g., /login/student, /login/staff, /login/admin, /login/parent).
The portal selection page is accessible from the public navigation bar and the homepage portal links.
Unauthenticated visitors can access this page without logging in.

requirements.text: Create four dedicated role-specific login pages — /login/student, /login/staff, /login/admin, and /login/parent — each branded with the role name and a short description (e.g., 'Student Portal — Access your courses, results, and fee statements'). Each page must show an 'Login with Internet Identity' button that triggers the Internet Identity login flow. After successful login, if the user already has a profile with the matching role, redirect them directly to their role dashboard. If the user has no profile yet, pre-select the matching role in the ProfileSetupModal (so the role dropdown defaults to the role corresponding to the login page they used) before prompting them to complete profile setup. This eliminates the need for the user to manually choose their role after logging in via a role-specific portal page.

quotes: admin will login as admin staff will login as staff student we login and student just as follows

acceptanceCriteria: Routes /login/student, /login/staff, /login/admin, and /login/parent each render a distinct role-branded login page.
Each login page displays the role name prominently and a brief portal description.
Clicking 'Login with Internet Identity' on any of these pages triggers the standard Internet Identity authentication flow.
After successful authentication, if a profile already exists with the correct role, the user is immediately redirected to their role's dashboard (/student, /staff, /admin, or /parent).
If no profile exists yet, the ProfileSetupModal opens with the role field pre-filled and locked to the role corresponding to the login page visited.
After profile setup is completed from a role-specific login page, the user is redirected to the correct role dashboard.

If an authenticated user with an existing mismatched role lands on a role-specific login page, show a message indicating they are already registered under a different role and provide a link to their actual dashboard.

requirements.text: Update the public homepage and the Navigation component so that the portal links (Student Portal, Staff Portal, Admin Portal, Parent Portal) that were previously pointing directly to dashboard routes now point to the corresponding role-specific login pages (/login/student, /login/staff, /login/admin, /login/parent) for unauthenticated visitors. For already-authenticated users with a confirmed role, these links should still navigate directly to their respective dashboards.

quotes: once a visitor clicks on their category either as a staff student admin or alsoever if you take them to a page where they would login as that portal access category

acceptanceCriteria: Homepage portal cards and navigation portal links route unauthenticated visitors to /login/{role} pages instead of directly to protected dashboard routes.
Authenticated users with a confirmed role clicking portal links are routed directly to their dashboards.
No broken links or 404 errors occur when navigating from portal entry points.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
The Internet Identity login flow must use the existing useInternetIdentity hook — do not replace or duplicate it.
Role-specific login pages must reuse the existing ProfileSetupModal component for new user onboarding; do not create a parallel profile setup flow.
All protected dashboard routes must remain guarded by the existing RoleGuard component.
nonGoals: Creating separate backend functions or authentication mechanisms per role — all authentication continues through the single Internet Identity provider.
Changing the existing ProfileSetupModal component internals beyond accepting a defaultRole prop.
Adding any new backend Motoko logic for this feature — all changes are frontend-only.
Redesigning the existing dashboard pages or sidebar navigation.
